# glitchtip_monitors (Data Source)

Lists all uptime monitors in a GlitchTip organization. Useful for iterating over existing monitors (e.g. with `for_each`) without enumerating them by hand.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List all uptime monitors in an organization.
data "glitchtip_monitors" "all" {
  organization = "acme-inc"
}

output "down_monitors" {
  value = [for m in data.glitchtip_monitors.all.monitors : m.name if m.is_up == false]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `organization` (String) Slug of the organization whose monitors are listed.

### Read-Only

- `monitors` (Attributes List) All uptime monitors in the organization. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--monitors))

<a id="nestedatt--monitors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `monitors`

Read-Only:

- `created` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp at which the monitor was created.
- `id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the monitor.
- `interval` (Number) Number of seconds between checks.
- `is_up` (Boolean) Whether the monitor is currently reporting the endpoint as up. Null until the first check runs.
- `monitor_type` (String) Type of check the monitor performs, e.g. `GET`, `POST`, `Ping`, `TCP Port`, `SSL`, or `Heartbeat`.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name of the monitor.
- `project_id` (String) Numeric ID of the project the monitor is attached to, or null for an organization-level monitor.
- `url` (String) Endpoint URL the monitor checks. Null for monitor types that have no URL.
